entries.evidence_summary: Authority is expressed as a highest-to-lowest operative chain, while static docs are separated under Reference evidence.
Material pushback is defined once in guardrails and reused verbatim in freeze and terminal-state criteria.
Outputs are split into canonical artifacts under tools/agent-role-builder/role/ and run-scoped evidence under tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<job-id>/ with explicit lifecycle labels.
Each terminal state has an explicit trigger and write consequence, including frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, blocked, and resume_required.
The self-check is limited to executed checks and uses semantic scope matching rather than literal string matching.
The not-in-scope list is deduplicated, normalized, and contains one exclusion per concept without contradictory variants.
Arbitration has a trigger, actor, required evidence locations, and a single allowed outcome of frozen_with_conditions.
Every canonical required output appears with its full tool-relative path; basename-only references were removed.
Completion is defined separately for frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, blocked, and resume_required with exact artifact expectations.
Context gathering is labeled as preconditions before Step 1 and not mixed into the numbered execution steps.
Carried-forward findings must be addressed by conceptual-group ID in the next fix-items map or explicitly rejected with reason.
Decision-log preservation is defined as append-only on update/fix, while the canonical board summary is replaced and the run snapshot is preserved.
The steps section declares that the workflow is target-state governed behavior, avoiding mixed current-vs-target ambiguity.
Diff summaries and run post-mortems are declared as every-round artifacts, including no-delta final rounds.
A single authoritative artifact matrix declares artifact class, full path, create lifecycle, update/fix lifecycle, and write rule for each artifact.
Local authority order and review semantics are derived from the COO/runtime contract, component review contract, and shared governance docs rather than reinvented locally.
The role mission is framed as a standing purpose for the package and does not describe a transient run state or recovery action.
The writable surface is explicitly bounded to four canonical files and the active run root, with no implied write authority elsewhere.
Only operative sources appear in the authority chain; request-time knobs are bounded below them and static docs are classified as reference evidence.
Governance mechanics are included only as inherited obligations or documented local consequences; no unsourced local board policy is introduced.
Scope polarity is cleanly separated: positive triggers appear under Use when, while exclusions appear only under Not in scope.
The governed round is defined as an ordered sub-sequence with compliance-map, fix-items-map, review, learning, diff-summary, and post-mortem artifacts bound to specific sub-steps.
The role declares component-local governance files and states which review obligations are inherited by reference from governing contracts and docs.
Required inputs enumerate request data, source evidence, reviewer roster, governance files, review mode, governance config, and runtime config needed for terminal decisions.
